quick_facts.label: Indian eligibility prerequisite
quick_facts.value: For Indian citizens and Overseas Citizens of India seeking admission to an overseas primary medical qualification (MBBS or equivalent), qualifying NEET-UG is mandatory; the NEET result is treated as the eligibility certificate when the prescribed MBBS-admission criteria are met.

quick_facts.label: India-practice standard
quick_facts.value: For students intending to obtain registration to practise allopathy in India, NMC’s Foreign Medical Graduate Licentiate (FMGL) framework requires compliance with prescribed standards for the foreign qualification; NMC warns that non-compliant study arrangements can make a graduate ineligible for Indian licensing examinations or registration.

quick_facts.label: Minimum foreign-study structure under FMGL guidance
quick_facts.value: NMC guidance identifies a minimum of 54 months of education in one foreign institution, English-medium instruction, required subjects, clinical training that is not split across countries, and a 12-month internship at the same foreign university for the FMGL route.

quick_facts.label: Indian screening examination
quick_facts.value: As of the June 2026 FMGE session, NBEMS conducts the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination (FMGE). Its eligibility page states that Indian citizens/OCIs with an appropriately recognised primary medical qualification may apply, subject to the session’s conditions and documentary requirements.

quick_facts.label: Online-study recordkeeping
quick_facts.value: NMC’s March 18, 2026 clarification addresses physical onsite compensation for periods of online study by foreign medical students; official transcripts and other authenticated records may be examined by the relevant State Medical Council when assessing compliance.

sections.heading: Who should pursue an overseas MBBS
paragraphs: For an Indian citizen or OCI, an MBBS-equivalent degree from outside India is not simply an overseas university admission choice if the eventual objective is medical practice in India. Qualifying NEET-UG is mandatory for admission to a foreign primary medical course, and the applicant must also meet the MBBS-admission eligibility conditions referenced in the NMC regulations.

Before enrolling, the student should assess the proposed institution and course against the Indian registration pathway—not only against the university’s offer letter. NMC has repeatedly cautioned students and parents that its FMGL conditions relating to duration, teaching medium, syllabus, clinical training and internship are mandatory for those seeking to practise allopathy in India.
bullets: Keep the NEET-UG result and admission records: NBEMS’s June 2026 FMGE documentation notice lists proof of qualifying NEET-UG, where applicable, alongside the admission letter or eligibility certificate.

Confirm that the foreign qualification is recognised for enrolment as a medical practitioner in the country where the awarding institution is located; NBEMS identifies this as an FMGE eligibility condition.
Do not rely solely on an agent’s or college’s recognition claims. NMC’s advisory directs prospective students to scrutinise compliance with the FMGL framework before admission.

sections.heading: Study length and internship requirements
paragraphs: For the FMGL route applicable to Indian registration, NMC guidance specifies at least 54 months of education in a single foreign institution and a 12-month internship completed at the same foreign university. The guidance also says clinical training must not be undertaken in parts across different countries.

NMC’s March 2026 clarification states that students admitted after November 18, 2021 are governed by the Compulsory Rotating Medical Internship Regulations, 2021 and are required to complete one year of mandatory internship in India as prescribed there. The precise sequence and documentation should be checked against the rules and notices applicable to the individual student’s admission date and record.
bullets: Treat advertised course length as insufficient by itself: check whether it includes the required foreign internship and whether the course is delivered through one institution.
Retain dated attendance, clinical-posting, internship, passport/travel and transcript records throughout the programme.
Where any part of teaching was conducted online, review NMC’s current physical-onsite-compensation clarification and retain the supporting academic records.

sections.heading: Curriculum and delivery safeguards
paragraphs: The overseas curriculum must be assessed for regulatory compatibility, not merely for the degree title. NMC’s foreign-medical-graduate guidance identifies English as the required medium of instruction and requires the prescribed mandatory subjects, practical and clinical training, and an internship/clinical clerkship arrangement consistent with the FMGL standards for an applicant seeking Indian registration.

A programme involving transfers, multiple teaching countries, remote-only study, or unclear clinical-posting arrangements carries particular regulatory risk. NMC’s guidance states that clinical training must not be split across countries, while its 2026 notices address physical onsite compensation and documentary assessment for periods undertaken online.
bullets: Obtain the complete year-by-year syllabus, assessment pattern and clinical-posting plan before paying fees.
Confirm in writing which campus, teaching hospital and country will deliver every academic and clinical component.
Ask the institution for official transcripts that record subjects, study duration, clinical postings and practical training; NMC’s 2026 FAQ identifies such records as relevant evidence.

sections.heading: Admission due diligence
paragraphs: Admission is made through the foreign university and is governed by that institution’s and host country’s rules, but Indian applicants who may later seek Indian registration should complete regulatory due diligence before accepting an offer. NMC has warned that some overseas institutions may not meet requirements on course duration, curriculum, training or infrastructure, and it places the responsibility for compliance on the student seeking the Indian licensing route.

A practical evidence file should start at admission. NBEMS’s June 2026 FMGE notice illustrates the kinds of records that may be needed later, including the primary medical qualification certificate, an eligibility certificate if applicable or admission letter/proof of NEET-UG qualification, proof of citizenship, and documents supporting transfers between institutions.
bullets: Verify the university’s legal authority to award the primary medical qualification and the qualification’s status for local medical registration.
Check that the entire course, clinical training and foreign internship meet the NMC conditions applicable to the planned Indian-registration route.
Keep original and authenticated copies of the offer letter, fee receipts, visas, passports, transcripts, clinical logbooks, internship certificate and final degree.

Fees, living costs, visa requirements and admission deadlines vary by country, institution and intake; obtain these directly from the university and host-country authorities in writing before committing funds.

sections.heading: Options after the foreign medical degree
paragraphs: For practice in India, the principal post-degree route is the Indian foreign-medical-graduate licensing pathway. As of the June 2026 session, NBEMS conducts FMGE; its published eligibility conditions include an appropriately recognised primary medical qualification and, where applicable, the eligibility certificate. NMC guidance additionally states that permanent registration requires a supervised internship in India for at least 12 months after application, subject to the applicable regulations and individual case.

For the United Kingdom, an overseas degree may lead to the GMC’s PLAB route if the primary medical qualification is acceptable. PLAB remains the test name and is compliant with the UK Medical Licensing Assessment framework; GMC states that applicants for full registration via this route need, among other things, an acceptable primary medical qualification, both PLAB parts within the preceding two years, and an internship.

For the United States, graduates are international medical graduates for ECFMG purposes. ECFMG states that certification requires passing USMLE Step 1 and Step 2 CK for the medical-science requirement, plus the clinical-skills and communication-skills requirements; ECFMG certification is also one eligibility requirement for USMLE Step 3. These are separate jurisdictional pathways, so a foreign degree does not itself guarantee licensure, residency placement or employment in India, the UK or the US.
bullets: India: review the current NBEMS FMGE bulletin and NMC internship/registration notices before planning an examination or internship timeline.
United Kingdom: check the individual degree’s acceptability directly with the GMC before booking PLAB; the GMC specifically advises against relying on third-party confirmation.
United States: confirm the medical school’s ECFMG eligibility and plan for ECFMG certification requirements before committing to the USMLE pathway.

Non-licensure options such as an MPH, clinical research, health policy or related roles have institution- and employer-specific admissions or hiring criteria; assess each programme or employer separately rather than assuming the degree alone is sufficient.
